tasks_completed: Cycle 1: lane partition in queue.py (_parse_card_program + enumerate_dispatchable(lane=)) and --lane/LOOP_LANE threading through daemon.sh + actions.py drain gate

Cycle 2: lib/claim.py (claim_brief/release_claim via refs/claims/<id> --force-with-lease empty-expect) + claim-before-worktree in actions.py dispatch() + release on 4 exit transitions + install.sh copy
tasks_remaining: Cycle 3: lib/tests/test_lane_and_claim.py — golden i (lane filter), golden ii (contention, load-bearing), golden iii (lane=None byte-for-byte equality)
learnings: queue.py: lane=None is the additive-invariant path — _parse_card_program is never called and the fingerprint string keeps its legacy 'goals_sig|ids' shape; a lane only adds a 'lane=<x>' namespace segment. Verified: 28 existing queue_test.py pass + golden i/iii spot-checks pass.

Fail-closed lane rule confirmed: a card with no Program: field is EXCLUDED from any lane-filtered enumeration (returns Program '' which never equals a real lane key). Tested with an unlabeled card absent from both alpha and beta.

daemon.sh: --lane is parsed from args-after-project_dir (shift then while-case) so the optional heartbeat positional still works in any order; CLI --lane wins over env/config LOOP_LANE; LOOP_LANE is exported so actions.py reads it via os.environ for the head_solo_drain gate. _LANE_OPT uses unquoted word-split (not an array) to dodge the bash-3.2 empty-array set -u trap — safe because lane names are space-free program slugs.

Cycle 2 anchor: dispatch() creates the worktree at the `ensure_worktree(paths, brief, branch, config)` call (actions.py ~line 1369, just after `git fetch`). claim_brief MUST be called BEFORE that line; on claim failure log + skip + create NO worktree.

Cycle 2 LOAD-BEARING git finding (verified live, git 2.50.1): claim_brief MUST push a UNIQUE minted commit object, NOT the shared HEAD. Pushing HEAD when the remote claim ref already points at HEAD makes git short-circuit to 'Everything up-to-date' (exit 0) WITHOUT evaluating --force-with-lease — so a 2nd claimer reading exit 0 wrongly believes it won (the brief's #1 escalation trigger: two True winners). Fix: _mint_claim_object uses `git mktree </dev/null` + `git -c user.name=... -c user.email=... commit-tree <empty-tree> -m 'claim <id> <host>:<pid>:<ns>'` so the pushed value always differs and the lease always fires. The empty-expect form is `--force-with-lease=refs/claims/<id>:` (trailing colon, no value).

Cycle 2: lease rejection prints '[rejected] ... (stale info)' to stderr and exits non-zero — that string is the ONLY non-zero outcome read as 'already claimed' (return False). Every other non-zero (auth/network/bad refspec) raises RuntimeError → dispatch fail-loud, no worktree. Verified: contention one-True/one-False, release+re-claim, and RuntimeError on a bogus file:// remote.

Cycle 2: release_claim wired via _release_claim_quiet (reads remote from config, imports claim by sys.path) at 4 active-exit transitions: merge(), reject_brief(), close_as_delivered(), move_to_awaiting_review(). Safe to release at awaiting_review because card Status stays 'active' there (only Status:queued is dispatchable) so no daemon re-grabs it; release makes a later human re-queue (Status→queued) re-claimable. Best-effort/non-fatal everywhere.

Cycle 2 verify: no .loop/config.sh in this project (no VERIFY_CMD); ran `python3 -m pytest lib/tests/ -q` = 150 passed (incl. test_dispatch_idempotency.py which exercises the modified dispatch path). Cycle 3 should add the contention suite + run test-flow-v2.sh as the regression gate.
